---
title: "Model Interface & Registry"
description: "MLModelOperation abstract interface, MLModelRegistry, task types, and parameters"
---

## Model Operation Interface

`MLModelOperation` (`models/MLModelOperation.hpp`) is the abstract base class for all ML algorithms. It provides a uniform API for both supervised and unsupervised workflows.

### Supervised Workflow

```cpp
auto model = registry.create("Random Forest");
auto params = model->getDefaultParameters();

model->train(features, labels, params.get());
model->predict(new_features, predictions);
// Optional:
model->predictProbabilities(new_features, probabilities);
```

### Unsupervised Workflow

```cpp
auto model = registry.create("K-Means");
auto params = model->getDefaultParameters();

model->fit(features, params.get());
model->assignClusters(new_features, assignments);
```

### Data Convention

All methods operate on armadillo matrices in **mlpack convention**:

- Feature matrices: `(features × observations)` — each **column** is one sample
- Label rows: `(1 × observations)` — each element is a class index `[0, num_classes)`
- Probability matrices: `(num_classes × observations)` — each column sums to 1.0

### Interface Methods

| Category | Method | Description |
|---|---|---|
| **Identity** | `getName()` | Human-readable name (e.g., "Random Forest") |
| | `getTaskType()` | `MLTaskType` enum value |
| | `getDefaultParameters()` | Factory for default parameter set |
| **Supervised** | `train(features, labels, params)` | Train on labeled data |
| | `predict(features, predictions)` | Predict class labels |
| | `predictProbabilities(features, probs)` | Per-class probability matrix |
| **Unsupervised** | `fit(features, params)` | Fit to unlabeled data |
| | `assignClusters(features, assignments)` | Assign cluster labels |
| **Persistence** | `save(ostream)` / `load(istream)` | Model serialization |
| **Query** | `isTrained()` | Whether model is ready for prediction |
| | `numClasses()` / `numFeatures()` | Training data dimensions |

Default implementations of supervised methods return `false` for unsupervised models (and vice versa), so each concrete model only overrides the methods relevant to its task type.

## Task Types

`MLTaskType` (`models/MLTaskType.hpp`) categorizes model operations:

```cpp
enum class MLTaskType {
    BinaryClassification,     // Two-class supervised
    MultiClassClassification, // N-class supervised (N ≥ 2)
    Clustering,               // Unsupervised grouping
};
```

The registry uses this to filter models when the UI selects a task type.

## Model Parameters

`MLModelParametersBase` (`models/MLModelParameters.hpp`) is the abstract base for algorithm-specific parameter sets. Each model has a concrete subclass:

### Supervised Parameters

| Struct | Parameters |
|---|---|
| `RandomForestParameters` | `num_trees` (10), `minimum_leaf_size` (1), `minimum_gain_split` (1e-7), `maximum_depth` (0 = unlimited) |
| `NaiveBayesParameters` | `epsilon` (1e-9) — variance smoothing |
| `LogisticRegressionParameters` | `lambda` (0.0), `step_size` (0.01), `max_iterations` (10000) |

### Unsupervised Parameters

| Struct | Parameters |
|---|---|
| `KMeansParameters` | `k` (3), `max_iterations` (1000), `seed` (optional) |
| `DBSCANParameters` | `epsilon` (1.0), `min_points` (5) |
| `GMMParameters` | `k` (3), `max_iterations` (300), `seed` (optional) |

## Model Registry

`MLModelRegistry` (`models/MLModelRegistry.hpp`) is a factory registry that stores factory functions for ML models. Each `create()` call returns a fresh, untrained model instance.

### Built-in Registration

The constructor registers all six built-in models. External code can register additional models via `registerModel()`.

### API

```cpp
MLModelRegistry registry;

// Query
auto all_names = registry.getAllModelNames();
auto classifiers = registry.getModelNames(MLTaskType::MultiClassClassification);
auto clusterers = registry.getModelNames(MLTaskType::Clustering);
bool has_rf = registry.hasModel("Random Forest");

// Create
auto model = registry.create("Random Forest");  // fresh, untrained instance
auto params = model->getDefaultParameters();

// Register custom model
registry.registerModel<MyCustomModel>();
// or:
registry.registerModel("My Model", MLTaskType::Clustering, []() {
    return std::make_unique<MyCustomModel>();
});
```

### Custom Model Registration

To add a new model:

1. Subclass `MLModelOperation` and implement the relevant methods
2. Create a corresponding parameter struct inheriting `MLModelParametersBase`
3. Register via `registerModel<T>()` or the three-argument overload

```cpp
class MyModel : public MLModelOperation {
public:
    std::string getName() const override { return "My Model"; }
    MLTaskType getTaskType() const override { return MLTaskType::Clustering; }
    std::unique_ptr<MLModelParametersBase> getDefaultParameters() const override {
        return std::make_unique<MyModelParameters>();
    }
    bool fit(arma::mat const & features, MLModelParametersBase const * params) override;
    bool assignClusters(arma::mat const & features, arma::Row<size_t> & assignments) override;
    bool isTrained() const override { return _trained; }
private:
    bool _trained = false;
};

// In the registry constructor or your registration code:
registry.registerModel<MyModel>();
```
